text.title: Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions
text.summary: Orchestration-based saga with compensation steps for creating an order across multiple services.

rule.statement: Every saga step must have a compensating action written before the happy path is merged. Compensation runs in reverse order when any step fails.
rule.rationale: A saga without compensations is an incomplete transaction. If step 2 fails after step 1 succeeds, step 1's side effect is permanent and cannot be undone.

rule.statement: Prefer orchestration (one central orchestrator) over choreography when more than 3-4 services participate. Choreography with many services creates spaghetti event flows that are impossible to debug.
rule.rationale: Orchestration centralizes the saga state machine and makes the flow readable as code. Choreography distributes it across services with no single place to observe the overall state.

example.note: Compensations run in reverse order. Failures in compensation are logged but not re-raised — best-effort rollback with alerting.
